hypothesis: Training template-free retrosynthesis models (e.g., RootAligned) with a loss re-weighted by the inverse square-root of template frequency will increase top-10 accuracy on templates with frequency 1–10 by at least 15 percentage points on the USPTO-retro test set while not decreasing top-5 round-trip accuracy by more than 2 percentage points, compared to the same model trained with standard (uniform) weighting.
